mnemonics.title: P and V Operations
mnemonics.content: P = Pass (wait/decrement) - Think of it as 'Passing through' requires permission, V = Verify (signal/increment) - Think of it as 'Victory' signal when done

mnemonics.title: Semaphore Types
mnemonics.content: Binary = Bike (2 wheels = 0 or 1), Counting = Carnival (many tickets/counts available)

mnemonics.title: Producer-Consumer
mnemonics.content: P-E-C: Producers wait on Empty, Consume, signal on Full; C-W-F: Consumers wait on Full, Wait, signal on Empty

visualTips.visualization: Draw a light switch - only ON(1) or OFF(0), one person can turn it on at a time
Draw a parking lot with N spaces - each car takes one space, when full, cars wait at entrance
Think of a ticket counter - when tickets = 0, you must wait in queue
Think of someone leaving a room and calling 'next' to let the next person in
Think of a restaurant kitchen - chefs (producers) fill plates, customers (consumers) take plates, limited rack (buffer) space

analogies.analogy: A bathroom key at a gym - only one person can use it at a time, others wait for the key to be returned
Restaurant reservation system with N tables - each reservation occupies one table, walk-ins must wait when all tables are taken
Writing your name on a shared whiteboard - only one person should write at a time to avoid messy/illegible results
Two people each holding one fork, both waiting for the other fork to eat - neither can proceed
